title: Bok Choy with Black Mushrooms

ingredients.title: Main
items: 500g baby bok choy (washed, halved lengthwise if large)

ingredients.title: Sauce
items: ½ tsp dark soy sauce (for color)
1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water (slurry)

instruction.title: 1. Rehydrate Mushrooms
steps: Soak dried black mushrooms in warm water for 30–45 minutes until soft.
Remove stems and slice if large. Reserve soaking liquid, strain and keep for sauce.

instruction.title: 2. Blanch Bok Choy
steps: Bring a pot of water to boil, add a pinch of salt.
Blanch bok choy for 30–45 seconds until bright green and tender-crisp.
Immediately transfer to ice water to stop cooking. Drain and set aside.

instruction.title: 3. Prepare Sauce
steps: In a small bowl, mix oyster sauce, soy sauces, sugar, and water/stock. Set aside.

instruction.title: 4. Stir-Fry Aromatics
steps: Heat oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and ginger, stir-fry for 20–30 seconds until fragrant.
Add black mushrooms and stir-fry for 1–2 minutes.

instruction.title: 5. Combine & Finish
steps: Add the blanched bok choy to the wok.
Pour in the sauce and toss gently to coat evenly.
Drizzle in cornstarch slurry while stirring, cook until sauce thickens and glazes vegetables.
Toss in scallions, give a final stir, and remove from heat.

instruction.title: 6. Plating
steps: Arrange bok choy neatly on a serving plate with mushrooms on top.
Spoon extra glossy sauce over vegetables for a restaurant-style finish.

chefTips: Use baby bok choy for a tender texture and elegant presentation.
Ensure high heat stir-frying to preserve color and crunch.
Mushroom soaking liquid can be used to enhance the umami depth of the sauce.
For a luxury version, drizzle a few drops of sesame oil or add a touch of Shaoxing wine while stir-frying.
